description: Dubai, located in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), is a global city known for its futuristic architecture, luxury shopping, and vibrant nightlife scene. It is home to the tallest building in the world, the Burj Khalifa, and boasts remarkable landmarks like the Palm Jumeirah, a man-made island, and the Burj Al Arab, often considered the world’s most luxurious hotel. Dubai is also a hub for business, finance, and tourism, attracting millions of visitors annually with its upscale resorts, world-class shopping malls, and events such as the Dubai Shopping Festival. The city’s unique blend of traditional Arabian culture and modern, cosmopolitan flair makes it an exciting and dynamic destination. Its strategic location also positions Dubai as a key gateway between the East and the West.
cost_includes:
Sightseeing:  
Half Day City Tour ( ATLANTIS , BURJ AL ARAB, FUTURE MUSEUM, PALM JUMERAH PHOTO STOP) 
Burj Khalifa with entrance to 124th floor (ATMOSPHERE LOUNGE WITH GOLD COFFEE) 
Marina Cruise with dinner   
Desert Safari, with Belly Dance and dinner 
Dubai Frame 
Return economy class airfare (EX BOM). 
Return airport transfers Dubai // Ras Al Khaimah/Abu Dhabi Airport depending as per flight details. 
Hotel accommodation in 4 star Hotel as per selected options with daily breakfast provided by hotel. 
Sightseeing mentioned with standard // basic entrance tickets. 
UAE normal 30 Days single entry tourist visa charges. 
Travel insurance for specific period. 
All tours & transfers on Private basis. 
Tourism Dirham tax.
cost_excludes:
GST 5% (Government Tax) on total package cost.
Porterage (Coolie charges), laundry, wines & alcoholic beverages, telephone charges, shopping, all items of personal nature and also food and drinks not forming the part of the group menus. 
Any extra expense such as route change, Airline change, Date change, Accommodation facilities, etc incurred due to the unforeseen, unavoidable forced majeure circumstances during the tour. 
Any unexpected cost arising due to natural calamities e.g. bad weather, landslides, road blockage, political disturbances (strikes), etc. (and has to be paid by travelers directly on the spot). 
Any extra cost incurred on behalf of an individual due to illness, accident, hospitalization, or any personal emergency.
Anything not specifically mentioned in ‘Tour Price Includes Column.

faqs.title: 1.)What is the best time to visit Dubai?
faqs.content: The best time to visit Dubai is between November and April when the weather is cooler and more pleasant, making it ideal for outdoor activities and sightseeing.

faqs.title: 2.)Do I need a visa to visit Dubai?
faqs.content: Yes, most travelers require a visa to visit Dubai, although citizens of certain countries are eligible for visa-on-arrival or visa-free entry. It's recommended to check specific visa requirements based on your nationality.

faqs.title: 3.)What currency is used in Dubai?
faqs.content: The official currency of Dubai is the United Arab Emirates Dirham (AED).

faqs.title: 4.)What are the must-see attractions in Dubai?
faqs.content: Must-see attractions include the Burj Khalifa, Palm Jumeirah, Dubai Mall, Dubai Fountain, Dubai Marina, and the desert safari. Don't miss the Dubai Museum and historic sites in Al Fahidi.

faqs.title: 5.)What should I wear in Dubai?
faqs.content: Dubai is a cosmopolitan city with modern attire commonly worn. However, visitors are advised to dress modestly in public spaces, particularly in religious sites or cultural areas.
